entities.name: KODO MILLET
entities.description: Paspalum scrobiculatum L., an underutilized nutrient-rich climate smart crop

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand drought conditions

entities.name: MARGINAL SOILS
entities.description: Soils with low productivity or poor quality where kodo millet is cultivated

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Diverse external factors affecting crop growth and nutrient expression

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the genetic makeup of crops for better yield and nutrition

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: DAYS TO MATURITY
entities.description: The time taken for the crop to reach maturity

entities.name: IRON CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of iron (Fe) in the grains

entities.name: ZINC CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of zinc (Zn) in the grains

entities.name: CALCIUM CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of calcium (Ca) in the grains

entities.name: PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of protein in the grains

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of variation in a trait that is due to genetic factors

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of the plant and the environment it is grown in

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The trait of reaching maturity in a shorter period

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of 100 seeds, used as an indicator of seed size

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The changing climate scenario affecting agricultural productivity

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific season during which the kodo millet accessions were evaluated

entities.name: ACCESSIONS
entities.description: Different samples or genotypes of kodo millet used for evaluation

entities.name: MULTILOCATION EVALUATION
entities.description: The process of evaluating genotypes across multiple locations to identify promising ones

relationships.description: Kodo millet is a drought-tolerant crop
Kodo millet is cultivated on marginal soils
Kodo millet is grown under diverse environmental conditions
Two hundred accessions of kodo millet were evaluated
Accessions were evaluated for variability in grain yield
Accessions were evaluated for variability in days to maturity
Accessions were evaluated for variability in iron content
Accessions were evaluated for variability in zinc content
Accessions were evaluated for variability in calcium content
Accessions were evaluated for variability in protein content
Promising accessions were identified for early maturity
Promising accessions were identified for greater 100-seed weight
Grain yield showed moderate to high heritability
Iron content showed moderate to high heritability
Zinc content showed moderate to high heritability
Calcium content showed moderate to high heritability
Protein content showed moderate to high heritability
Iron content is sensitive to genotype x environment interaction
Zinc content is sensitive to genotype x environment interaction
Calcium content is sensitive to genotype x environment interaction
Protein content is sensitive to genotype x environment interaction
Identified accessions are used for crop improvement
Crop improvement contributes to food and nutritional security
Kodo millet is a climate smart crop for changing climate scenarios
Multilocation evaluation is used to identify promising accessions
Multilocation evaluation supports crop improvement
Accessions were evaluated during the rainy season
